name: Banana, raw (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw, ripe pulp)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free glucose; one of the two dominant free sugars in ripe banana.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Free fructose; co-dominant free sugar with glucose.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Rises then falls during ripening; third major sugar.

components.name: starch
components.evidence: Highly ripeness-dependent: ~20-25 g/100g in green banana, falling to ~1-5 g/100g when fully ripe as it is hydrolysed to free sugars. Value shown is for typical retail (partly ripe) banana.

components.name: total sugars
components.evidence: Sum of free sugars; listed for reference (individual sugars above are what microbes consume).

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Includes pectin and resistant starch; largely non-fermentable by simple chassis but relevant for gut microbes.

components.name: malic acid
components.source: Etienne et al. 2013 Sci Hortic doi:10.1016/j.scienta.2013.08.005; Wyman & Palmer 1964 Plant Physiol 39:630; Maduwanthi & Marapana 2019 J Food Qual doi:10.1155/2019/7653154
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid of ripe banana (~60-65% of titratable acidity); rises 3-7 fold during ripening. Literature midpoint ~0.25-0.5 g/100g.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Etienne et al. 2013; Wyman & Palmer 1964 Plant Physiol 39:630
components.evidence: Second most abundant organic acid (~20% of acidity); most abundant acid at the green stage, declines relative to malic on ripening.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Maduwanthi & Marapana 2019 (green-stage measured 30.8 mg/100g); dietary-oxalate reviews
components.evidence: Contributes ripe-fruit astringency (~10% of acidity); higher in green fruit (~30 mg/100g) and declines on ripening; ripe midpoint ~6 mg/100g.

components.name: tartaric acid
components.evidence: Minor acid; measured value is green-stage; likely lower in fully ripe fruit.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: Present as a minor acid, not reliably quantified for ripe pulp.

components.name: protein
components.evidence: Total protein; amino-acid profile below is protein-derived.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Protein-bound (aspartic acid); abundant free + bound in banana.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Protein-bound (glutamic acid); most abundant amino acid.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-bound; corrects the current 'D-serine' entry (banana protein contains L-serine).

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Banana is very low-fat; fatty acids below are the measurable fraction.

components.name: pyridoxine (B6)
components.evidence: Vitamin B6; banana is a notably rich source.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present in trace amounts; not quantified by USDA. Banana biotin is low (~few ug/100g).

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: Retinol activity equivalents, entirely from provitamin-A carotenoids (no preformed retinol).

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Total iron (consolidates the redundant Fe2+/Fe3+/iron entries in the current list).

components.name: molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: Genuine ultratrace essential; not quantified by USDA. Kept.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion present; not quantified. Kept for ionic balance.

components.name: gallic acid
components.evidence: Genuine phenolic acid; part of total phenolics 38-128 mg GAE/100g.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed retinol (animal-form vitamin A) is implausible in a plant food; banana's vitamin A activity comes entirely from provitamin-A carotenoids (alpha-/beta-carotene). FooDB predicted/mis-assigned.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Wrong stereochemistry: banana protein contains L-serine, not the D-enantiomer. FooDB artifact; replaced by L-serine (0.04 g/100g).

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: Ultratrace contaminant element, not a defined growth nutrient of banana; below nutritional relevance.

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: Cobalt ion at ultratrace level; not a defined banana nutrient (banana has no B12). Also collides in name-space with CO2. Drop as noise.

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: Ultratrace element, not nutritionally quantified or defining for banana; drop as trace noise.

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: Iodine in banana is negligible/trace and not a growth-relevant nutrient; malformed 'Iodine c' name indicates FooDB noise.

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+) / Fe2+ mitochondria (duplicate iron entries)
remove.reason: Redundant compartment/oxidation-state duplicates of iron; consolidated into a single 'iron' component (0.26 mg/100g).

missing_added: malic acid (0.325 g/100g) — dominant organic acid, previously absent
quantitative amounts for D-glucose, D-fructose, sucrose, maltose, starch
L-valine (0.047 g/100g) and L-phenylalanine (0.049 g/100g) — missing amino acids
quantitative amounts for all vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids
dietary fiber, total protein, total fat
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a growth medium: free sugars (glucose approx fructose > sucrose, together ~12 g/100g) plus ripeness-dependent starch (high in green, hydrolysed to sugars when ripe), with malic acid the dominant organic acid (~0.3 g/100g, ~60-65% of titratable acidity) followed by citric acid. Composition is strongly ripeness-dependent: green fruit is starch-rich and more acidic, ripe fruit is free-sugar-rich. Signature mineral is potassium (358 mg/100g); banana is a rich B6 source. Removed FooDB noise: preformed retinol, D-serine stereochem error, ultratrace Ni/Co/Cr/I, and redundant iron entries.
